conflicts.description: Chapter 3 says the Takara sighting submission hit the system three weeks before the Bebop returned, chapter 9 ties the bounty wake-up to an Europa archive query eleven months earlier, and chapter 14 says the sighting report used as bait was filed two days before the Bebop came home.
conflicts.canonical_value: Ren's broader file research reaches back at least eleven months, but the operative Takara sighting report used to put the bounty in front of the Bebop was filed two days before they came home.
conflicts.resolution_note: Ed's full reveal in chapter 14 makes the two-day filing integral to Ren's bait plan and docking-pattern timing. The earlier Europa material is kept as research history; the 'three weeks' line is treated as the stray timing mismatch.

conflicts.description: The prior continuity sheet estimated two to three weeks, but the manuscript's own day markers compress the on-page action to roughly ten days.
conflicts.canonical_value: The on-page story spans about ten days, with one overnight at Dry Well leading directly into chapter 18.
conflicts.resolution_note: Successive 'night before,' 'two days ago,' and 'yesterday' markers tighten the early chapters, and chapters 17-18 cover one overnight plus same-day departure/return. That is materially shorter than the previous two-to-three-week estimate.
